contents: From HoTT Require Import Basics Types Truncations

From HoTT Require Import Basics Types Truncations
  Pointed.Core Pointed.pEquiv Pointed.Loops Pointed.pModality.
From HoTT.WildCat Require Import Core Equiv.

(** * Truncations of pointed types *)

(** TODO: Many things here can be generalized to any modality or any reflective subuniverse, and could be moved to pModality.v *)

Definition pTr (n : trunc_index) (A : pType) : pType
  := [Tr n A, _].
(** We specialize [pto] and [pequiv_pto] from pModalities.v to truncations. *)

Definition ptr {n : trunc_index} {A : pType} : A ->* pTr n A
  := pto (Tr n) _.

Definition pequiv_ptr {n : trunc_index} {A : pType} `{IsTrunc n A}
  : A <~>* pTr n A := @pequiv_pto (Tr n) A _.

(** We could specialize [pO_rec] to give the following result, but since maps induced by truncation-recursion compute on elements of the form [tr _], we can give a better proof of pointedness than the one coming from [pO_rec]. *)

Definition pTr_rec n {X Y : pType} `{IsTrunc n Y} (f : X ->* Y)
  : pTr n X ->* Y
  := Build_pMap (Trunc_rec f) (point_eq f).
(** Note that we get an equality of pointed functions here, without Funext, while [pO_rec_beta] only gives a pointed homotopy. This is because [pTr_rec] computes on elements of the form [tr _]. *)

Definition pTr_rec_beta_path n {X Y : pType} `{IsTrunc n Y} (f : X ->* Y)
  : pTr_rec n f o* ptr = f.

goals.conclusion: pTr_rec n f o* ptr = f
pTr_rec n f o* ptr = f
Build_pMap (fun x : X => f x) (1 @ point_eq f) = f

contents: (* Since [f] is definitionally equal to [Build_pMap _ _ f (point_eq f)], this works: *)

goals.conclusion: 1 @ point_eq f = dpoint_eq f

contents: (** The version with a pointed homotopy. *)

Definition pTr_rec_beta n {X Y : pType} `{IsTrunc n Y} (f : X ->* Y)
  : pTr_rec n f o* ptr ==* f
  := phomotopy_path (pTr_rec_beta_path n f).
(** A pointed version of the induction principle. *)

Definition pTr_ind n {X : pType} {Y : pFam (pTr n X)} `{forall x, IsTrunc n (Y x)}
  (f : pForall X (Build_pFam (Y o tr) (dpoint Y)))
  : pForall (pTr n X) Y
  := Build_pForall (pTr n X) Y (Trunc_ind Y f) (dpoint_eq f).

goals.conclusion: Is0Functor (pTr n)
Is0Functor (pTr n)
forall a b : pType, (a $-> b) -> pTr n a $-> pTr n b
pTr n X $-> pTr n Y
Is1Functor (pTr n)
Is1Functor (pTr n)

forall (a b : pType) (f g : a $-> b),
f $== g -> fmap (pTr n) f $== fmap (pTr n) g
forall a : pType, fmap (pTr n) (Id a) $== Id (pTr n a)

forall (a b c : pType) (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> c),
fmap (pTr n) (g $o f) $== fmap (pTr n) g $o fmap (pTr n) f

forall (a b : pType) (f g : a $-> b),
f $== g -> fmap (pTr n) f $== fmap (pTr n) g

contents: intros X Y f g p.

goals.conclusion: fmap (pTr n) f $== fmap (pTr n) g

forall x : X,
{|
  pfam_pr1 := fun x0 : X => tr (f x0) = tr (g x0);
  dpoint := (ap tr (point_eq f) @ 1) @ (ap tr (point_eq g) @ 1)^
|} x

forall x : X,
{|
  pfam_pr1 := fun x0 : X => tr (f x0) = tr (g x0);
  dpoint := (ap tr (point_eq f) @ 1) @ (ap tr (point_eq g) @ 1)^
|} x
forall x : X, tr (f x) = tr (g x)

((fun x : X => ap tr (p x))
 :
 forall x : X,
 {|
   pfam_pr1 := fun x0 : X => tr (f x0) = tr (g x0);
   dpoint := (ap tr (point_eq f) @ 1) @ (ap tr (point_eq g) @ 1)^
 |} x) pt =
dpoint
  {|
    pfam_pr1 := fun x : X => tr (f x) = tr (g x);
    dpoint := (ap tr (point_eq f) @ 1) @ (ap tr (point_eq g) @ 1)^
  |}
ap tr (p point0) = (ap tr (p point0 @ 1) @ 1) @ 1

hypotheses.type: forall x : X, f x = g x

goals.conclusion: forall a : pType, fmap (pTr n) (Id a) $== Id (pTr n a)
fmap (pTr n) (Id X) $== Id (pTr n X)
fmap (pTr n) (Id X) == Id (pTr n X)
?p pt = dpoint_eq (fmap (pTr n) (Id X)) @ (dpoint_eq (Id (pTr n X)))^

Trunc_rec_tr n pt =
dpoint_eq (fmap (pTr n) (Id X)) @ (dpoint_eq (Id (pTr n X)))^

forall (a b c : pType) (f : a $-> b) (g : b $-> c),
fmap (pTr n) (g $o f) $== fmap (pTr n) g $o fmap (pTr n) f

contents: intros X Y Z f g.

goals.conclusion: fmap (pTr n) (g $o f) $== fmap (pTr n) g $o fmap (pTr n) f
fmap (pTr n) (g $o f) == fmap (pTr n) g $o fmap (pTr n) f

?p pt =
dpoint_eq (fmap (pTr n) (g $o f)) @
(dpoint_eq (fmap (pTr n) g $o fmap (pTr n) f))^

Trunc_ind
  (fun aa : Trunc n X =>
   fmap (pTr n) (g $o f) aa = (fmap (pTr n) g $o fmap (pTr n) f) aa)
  (fun a : X => 1) pt =
dpoint_eq (fmap (pTr n) (g $o f)) @
(dpoint_eq (fmap (pTr n) g $o fmap (pTr n) f))^

contents: (** Naturality of [ptr].  Note that we get a equality of pointed functions, not just a pointed homotopy. *)

Definition ptr_natural_path (n : trunc_index) {X Y : pType} (f : X ->* Y)
  : fmap (pTr n) f o* ptr = ptr o* f
  := pTr_rec_beta_path n (ptr o* f).
(** The version with a pointed homotopy. *)

Definition ptr_natural (n : trunc_index) {X Y : pType} (f : X ->* Y)
  : fmap (pTr n) f o* ptr ==* ptr o* f
  := phomotopy_path (ptr_natural_path n f).

Definition ptr_functor_pconst {X Y : pType} n
  : fmap (pTr n) (@pconst X Y) ==* pconst.

goals.conclusion: fmap (pTr n) pconst ==* pconst
fmap (pTr n) pconst ==* pconst
fmap (pTr n) pconst == pconst
?p pt = dpoint_eq (fmap (pTr n) pconst) @ (dpoint_eq pconst)^

Trunc_ind (fun aa : Trunc n X => fmap (pTr n) pconst aa = pconst aa)
  (fun a : X => 1) pt =
dpoint_eq (fmap (pTr n) pconst) @ (dpoint_eq pconst)^

contents:
Definition pequiv_ptr_functor {X Y : pType} (n : trunc_index) (f : X <~>* Y)
  : pTr n X <~>* pTr n Y
  := emap (pTr n) f.

Definition ptr_loops `{Univalence} (n : trunc_index) (A : pType)
  : pTr n (loops A) <~>* loops (pTr n.+1 A).

goals.conclusion: pTr n (loops A) <~>* loops (pTr n.+1 A)
pTr n (loops A) <~>* loops (pTr n.+1 A)
pTr n (loops A) <~> loops (pTr n.+1 A)
?f pt = pt
equiv_path_Tr pt pt pt = pt

contents:
Definition ptr_iterated_loops `{Univalence} (n : trunc_index)
  (k : nat) (A : pType)
  : pTr n (iterated_loops k A) <~>* iterated_loops k (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n k) A).

goals.conclusion:
pTr n (iterated_loops k A) <~>*
iterated_loops k (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n k) A)

pTr n (iterated_loops k A) <~>*
iterated_loops k (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n k) A)

forall (A : pType) (n : trunc_index),
pTr n (iterated_loops k A) <~>*
iterated_loops k (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n k) A)

forall (A : pType) (n : trunc_index),
pTr n (iterated_loops 0 A) <~>*
iterated_loops 0 (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n 0) A)

forall (A : pType) (n : trunc_index),
pTr n (iterated_loops k.+1 A) <~>*
iterated_loops k.+1 (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n k.+1) A)

hypotheses.type:
forall (A : pType) (n : trunc_index),
pTr n (iterated_loops k A) <~>*
iterated_loops k (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n k) A)

goals.conclusion:
forall (A : pType) (n : trunc_index),
pTr n (iterated_loops 0 A) <~>*
iterated_loops 0 (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n 0) A)
pTr n A <~>* pTr n A

forall (A : pType) (n : trunc_index),
pTr n (iterated_loops k.+1 A) <~>*
iterated_loops k.+1 (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n k.+1) A)

hypotheses.type:
forall (A : pType) (n : trunc_index),
pTr n (iterated_loops k A) <~>*
iterated_loops k (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n k) A)

goals.conclusion:
pTr n (iterated_loops k.+1 A) <~>*
iterated_loops k.+1 (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n k.+1) A)

hypotheses.type:
forall (A0 : pType) (n0 : trunc_index),
pTr n0 (iterated_loops k A0) <~>*
iterated_loops k (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n0 k) A0)

goals.conclusion:
pTr n
  (loops
     ((fix F (m : nat) : pType :=
         match m with
         | 0%nat => A
         | m'.+1%nat => loops (F m')
         end)
        k)) <~>*
?Goal

pTr n0 (iterated_loops k A0) <~>*

?Goal <~>*
loops

      | m'.+1%nat => loops (F m')

pTr n0 (iterated_loops k A0) <~>*
iterated_loops k (pTr (trunc_index_inc' n0 k) A0)

loops

(fix F (m : nat) : pType :=

contents:
Definition ptr_loops_eq `{Univalence} (n : trunc_index) (A : pType)
  : pTr n (loops A) = loops (pTr n.+1 A) :> pType
  := path_ptype (ptr_loops n A).
(* This lemma generalizes a goal that appears in [ptr_loops_commutes], allowing us to prove it by path induction. *)

Definition path_Tr_commutes (n : trunc_index) (A : Type) (a0 a1 : A) (p : a0 = a1)
  : path_Tr (n:=n) (tr p) = ap tr p.

goals.conclusion: path_Tr (tr p) = ap tr p
path_Tr (tr p) = ap tr p

contents:
Definition ptr_loops_commutes `{Univalence} (n : trunc_index) (A : pType)
  : (ptr_loops n A) o* ptr ==* fmap loops ptr.

goals.conclusion: ptr_loops n A o* ptr ==* fmap loops ptr
ptr_loops n A o* ptr ==* fmap loops ptr
ptr_loops n A o* ptr == fmap loops ptr
?p pt = dpoint_eq (ptr_loops n A o* ptr) @ (dpoint_eq (fmap loops ptr))^
ptr_loops n A o* ptr == fmap loops ptr
(ptr_loops n A o* ptr) p = fmap loops ptr p
Descent.path_OO (Tr n.+1) (Tr n) pt pt (tr p) = 1 @ (ap tr p @ 1)
Descent.path_OO (Tr n.+1) (Tr n) pt pt (tr p) = ?Goal
?Goal = 1 @ (ap tr p @ 1)
Descent.path_OO (Tr n.+1) (Tr n) pt pt (tr p) = ?Goal
ap tr p = 1 @ (ap tr p @ 1)
1 @ (ap tr p @ 1) = ?Goal
?Goal = ap tr p
1 @ (ap tr p @ 1) = ?Goal
ap tr p @ 1 = ap tr p

((fun p : loops A =>
  path_Tr_commutes n A pt pt p @
  (concat_1p (ap tr p @ 1) @ concat_p1 (ap tr p))^
  :
  (ptr_loops n A o* ptr) p = fmap loops ptr p)
 :
 ptr_loops n A o* ptr == fmap loops ptr) pt =
dpoint_eq (ptr_loops n A o* ptr) @ (dpoint_eq (fmap loops ptr))^

contents:
Definition pequiv_ptr_prod (n : trunc_index) (A B : pType)
  : pTr n (A * B) <~>* pTr n A * pTr n B.

goals.conclusion: pTr n (A * B) <~>* pTr n A * pTr n B
pTr n (A * B) <~>* pTr n A * pTr n B
pTr n (A * B) <~> [pTr n A * pTr n B, ispointed_prod]
?f pt = pt
equiv_Trunc_prod_cmp n pt = pt

contents:
(** ** Truncatedness of [pForall] and [pMap] *)

(** Buchholtz-van Doorn-Rijke, Theorem 4.2:  Let [j >= -1] and [n >= -2].  When [X] is [j]-connected and [Y] is a pointed family of [j+n+1]-truncated types, the type of pointed sections is [n]-truncated.  We formalize it with [j] replaced with a trunc index [m.+1] to enforce [j >= -1]. This version also allows [n] to be one smaller than BvDR allow. *)

goals.conclusion: IsTrunc n (pForall X Y)

hypotheses.type: forall x : X, IsTrunc (n +2+ m) (Y x)

goals.conclusion: IsTrunc n (pForall X Y)

hypotheses.type: forall x : X, IsTrunc (n +2+ m) (Y x)

goals.conclusion: IsTrunc n (Extensions.ExtensionAlong (unit_name pt) Y (unit_name (dpoint Y)))

hypotheses.type: forall x : X, IsTrunc (n +2+ m) (Y x)

contents: (** From this we deduce the non-dependent version, which is Corollary 4.3 of BvDR.  We include [n = -2] here as well, but in this case it is not interesting.  Since [X ->* Y] is inhabited, the [n = -1] case also gives contractibility, with weaker hypotheses. *)

Definition istrunc_pmap `{Univalence} {m n : trunc_index} (X Y : pType)
  `{!IsConnected m.+1 X} `{!IsTrunc (n +2+ m) Y}
  : IsTrunc n (X ->* Y)
  := istrunc_pforall X (pfam_const Y).

(** We can give a different proof of the [n = -1] case (with the conclusion upgraded to contractibility).  This proof works with [Tr (m.+1)] replaced with any reflective subuniverse [O] and doesn't require univalence.  Is it possible to generalize this to dependent functions while still avoiding univalence and/or keeping [O] a general RSU or modality?  Can [istrunc_pmap] be proven without univalence?  What about [istrunc_pforall]?  If the [n = -2] or [n = -1] cases can be proven without univalence, the rest can be done inductively without univalence. *)

goals.conclusion: Contr (X ->* Y)
Contr (X ->* Y)
([O X, ispointed_O X] ->* Y) <~> (X ->* Y)

contents:
Instance is_minus_one_connected_pointed (X : pType)
  : IsConnected (Tr (-1)) X
  := contr_inhabited_hprop _ (tr pt).
